dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7672 IH: Parity for Pacific Radiation Survivors Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Moylan introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend the Radiation Exposure Compensation Act to include Guam.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Parity for Pacific Radiation Survivors Act.
Section 4(b)(1) of the Radiation Exposure Compensation Act (Public Law 101–426; 42 U.S.C. 2210 note) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in the matter preceding clause (i), by striking subparagraph (B) and inserting subparagraphs (B) and (C);
in clause (ii), by striking and at the end; and
by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: with respect to a claim by an individual under subsection (a)(1)(A)(i)(IV) or (a)(2)(D), only Guam.

section.header: Leukemia claims relating to tests in the pacific
section.text: Section 4(a)(1)(A) of the Radiation Exposure Compensation Act (Public Law 101–426; 42 U.S.C. 2210 note) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in subclause (III), by striking or at the end;
by redesignating subclause (IV) as subclause (VI); and
by inserting after subclause (III) the following:

subclause.text: was physically present in an affected area—

item.text: for a period of at least 1 year during the period beginning on July 1, 1946, and ending on November 6, 1992; or
for the period beginning on April 25, 1962, and ending on November 6, 1962;

subclause.text: was a member of the Armed Forces who participated onsite in the radiological cleanup of Enewetak Atoll for any period of time during the period beginning on May 1, 1977, and ending on May 31, 1980; or

paragraph.text: in clause (ii), by striking subclause (I), (II), or (III) of clause (i) or onsite participation described in clause (i)(IV) and inserting subclause (I), (II), (III), or (IV) of clause (i) or onsite participation described in subclause (V) or (VI) of clause (i).

section.header: Specified diseases claims relating to tests in the pacific

subsection.text: Section 4(a)(2) of the Radiation Exposure Compensation Act (Public Law 101–426; 42 U.S.C. 2210 note) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (C), by striking or at the end;
by redesignating subparagraph (D) as subparagraph (F); and
by inserting after subparagraph (C) the following:

subparagraph.text: was physically present in an affected area—

clause.text: for a period of at least 1 year during the period beginning on July 1, 1946, and ending on November 21, 1992; or
for the period beginning on April 25, 1962, and ending on November 6, 1962;

subparagraph.text: was a member of the Armed Forces who participated onsite in the radiological cleanup of Enewetak Atoll for any period of time during the period beginning on May 1, 1977, and ending on May 31, 1980; or

subsection.text: Section 4(b)(2) of the Radiation Exposure Compensation Act (Public Law 101–426; 42 U.S.C. 2210 note) is amended—

paragraph.text: by inserting renal disease, after lymphomas (other than Hodgkin's disease),; and
by striking or lung and inserting lung, or renal.
